response.justification: Strong geospatial domain knowledge and proven technical depth across diverse systems, but the 11-year gap since last C++ work (2014-2015) and lack of navigation routing experience create significant risk for a Mid-Senior full-time IC role. Recent career trajectory toward web/full-stack engineering suggests movement away from systems programming, and no evidence of production routing systems, traffic integration, or related domain expertise.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise from founding GISCollective and Green Map work—rare and directly relevant to routing tile systems
Demonstrated ability to learn diverse languages and technologies across systems, web, and embedded domains
Strong software craftsmanship mindset with TDD focus and track record at high-performance companies (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
concerns: No C++ production experience for 11 years; last role was 2014-2015 at National Instruments, creating substantial ramp-up risk for a C++ systems role
No navigation or routing systems experience; geospatial background is data collection and platform-focused, not routing optimization, tile generation, or traffic integration
Recent career direction (re:cinq TypeScript/Node.js, Optilyz pricing platform) indicates focus on web/full-stack rather than systems programming
